sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: 1. FC Union Berlin have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses, including a strong 4-3 victory over Eintracht Frankfurt. Borussia M'gladbach, on the other hand, are struggling, with no wins in their last 5 league games (3 draws, 2 losses), conceding heavily in a 6-4 defeat to Frankfurt. Union’s recent form gives them a slight edge at home.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Borussia M'gladbach have had the upper hand, winning both matches (1-0 at home and 2-1 away). Their recent dominance in direct matchups could provide a psychological boost despite their poor league form this season.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Union Berlin, sitting 13th with 7 points, have struggled defensively, conceding 13 goals (15th in the league), but rank well in creating big chances (7th). M'gladbach, languishing in 17th with just 3 points, have scored only 5 goals while conceding 12, indicating issues at both ends of the pitch. Union’s slightly better metrics suggest they may capitalize on Gladbach’s frailties.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Despite Sofascore’s prediction favoring an away win (42%) over a home win (31%) or draw (27%), Union Berlin’s home advantage at An der alten Försterei and Gladbach’s dismal form tilt the balance slightly towards the hosts. Expect a competitive match with potential for goals, given both teams’ defensive vulnerabilities.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, 1. FC Union Berlin leads Borussia M'gladbach with a score of 2-1 at An der alten Försterei. Union Berlin has been clinical in front of goal, capitalizing on their opportunities early in the match. Danilho Doekhi has been the standout performer for the home side, finding the net twice with headers. On the other hand, Borussia M'gladbach managed to pull one back through Haris Tabaković, keeping them in contention despite trailing.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance comparison
halftimeSections.text: Union Berlin has shown efficiency in attack despite having less ball possession compared to M'gladbach. Their ability to create big chances and convert them has been key to their lead. M'gladbach, with a dominant share of possession, has struggled to break through Union’s defense consistently, though they’ve had their moments in the final third. Both teams have shown equal physicality in duels, but Union’s aerial dominance has given them an edge in set-piece situations.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Union Berlin start with aggressive attacking spells, particularly in the opening minutes, where they quickly established dominance. Their momentum continued with notable surges, leading to their goals. M'gladbach, after initially being on the back foot, shifted the momentum in their favor during the middle of the half, showing persistent pressure in Union’s half. Towards the end of the half, the away side maintained a stronger attacking presence, though they couldn’t level the score before the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Union Berlin, Danilho Doekhi has been a revelation, not only scoring twice but also posing a constant threat in the box. His aerial ability could be crucial in the second half. Christopher Trimmel has also been instrumental with his assist and key passes. On M'gladbach’s side, Haris Tabaković remains their biggest threat up front, having already scored, while Rocco Reitz’s creativity in midfield could unlock Union’s defense with his ability to deliver incisive passes.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to see M'gladbach push harder for an equalizer, given their possession dominance in the first half. Union Berlin, however, will aim to protect their lead by staying compact and looking for counter-attacking opportunities. Set-pieces could again play a significant role for the home side, while M'gladbach will need to be more clinical with their entries into the penalty area. Both teams are expected to create chances, and it could come down to which defense holds firm under pressure.
